scope: Tencent Video main-interface parameter closure canonical inventory refreshed through 2026-06-23 evidence, including the later caller-facing shell validations, appid upper-edge cutovers, and anonymous real-cookie replay.
method: Use repository evidence only: docs, examples, contract probes, environment matrix, and focused JSONP closure artifacts.
Do not upgrade guesses about official backend naming into conclusions.
Mark unresolved items as open questions rather than filling them with inference.

tid.observed_role: request routing / config selector; `431` is the current canonical caller-default, while later anonymous direct-call evidence now separates API1 into a canonical branch plus several thinner shell families rather than one flat positive class

failing_examples.errormsg: 错误的tid, 如果新申请的接口,请等待10分钟
错误的tid, 如果新申请的接口,请等待10分钟
错误的tid, 如果新申请的接口,请等待10分钟

tid.environment_status: same-day stable on the current anonymous matrix; later direct-call validation tightened the caller-facing family map to `431/453/476/483/506/537`, but aged-cookie/login-state replay is still open for these shells.
notes: Repeated `tid` currently behaves as first-value-wins in the tested canonical anonymous branch.

Later caller-facing validation now distinguishes `453` as a positive cover-shell-only branch, `483` as a video_ids-led thin success shell, `476` as an imgtag-family ultra-thin shell, `506` as an empty-valued field shell, and `537` as another thinner success-shell family for branch probing.

Broader API1 slices around 418-421, 446-455, 456-521, and 526-536 only tightened local boundaries around the currently known family map; they did not reveal another positive-detail branch outside `431` or another confirmed positive cover-shell family outside `453`.
The later anonymous 641-647 jump-scan also found no new API1 positive branch; the strongest new detail there is a local `tid=642 -> -111013` wrinkle on the second sample, not a new positive family.
open_questions: Whether API1 has another positive or success-shell tid beyond the current `431/453/476/483/506/537` family map is still not closed.

batch_semantics: Supports multiple CIDs in one request.
Duplicate CIDs count toward the tested non-empty item limit.
Empty comma slots are ignored and do not count toward the tested non-empty item limit.
32 non-empty items succeed in the current matrix; 33 non-empty items trigger `-111001 / idlist个数错误, 为0或超过限制`.
Mixed valid + illegal CID can still return top-level success; callers must inspect per-result retcode.
Shape-valid but empty-shell CID can pollute naive top-level field reads when it appears first.
Repeated query-key form such as `idlist=a&idlist=b` currently behaves as first-value-wins in the tested canonical anonymous branch.
open_questions: No evidence yet that API1 merges repeated `idlist` keys across query-string positions.

appid.observed_role: numeric canonical appid enables the stable documented appkey-validation branch; current anonymous numeric evidence is at least three-way rather than simple canonical-vs-other: `1` still rides a looser success-bypass branch, far noncanonical numerics land on `appid no find`, and the searched near-canonical band lands on `appkey error`.
notes: `appid=10001005` is the current canonical numeric appid in repo examples and probes.
Current evidence says API1 appid is not a simple always-required auth parameter.
Repeated `appid` currently behaves as first-value-wins in the tested bad-appkey collision controls between the canonical numeric branch and the text-bypass branch.
The searched anonymous cutovers are now tightened to `10001000 -> appid no find`, `10001001 -> appkey error`, `10001186 -> appkey error`, and `10001187 -> appid no find`.
open_questions: The tested anonymous numeric slice is now partially mapped, but the full numeric appid space is still not closed outside the searched cutovers and bands.

appkey.observed_role: part of the stable documented branch when the canonical numeric appid is used
working_examples: paired with `appid=10001005` and the canonical appkey
omitted when appid is missing in the tested anonymous branch
omitted when appid is text in the tested anonymous branch
notes: Repeated `appkey` currently behaves as first-value-wins in the tested canonical-appid branch when good and wrong appkey values collide.
Current anonymous numeric evidence still does not show that the canonical appkey satisfies the searched near-canonical appid family `10001001-10001186`; those values remain on `appkey error`.
open_questions: No evidence yet that appkey alone unlocks additional API1 capability beyond satisfying the exact canonical numeric-appid branch.

current_non_goals: Current canonical anonymous-branch evidence says API1 ignores the tested extra keys `foo`, `callback`, and `_`, and still shows no wrapper-style behavior from them.
priority_gaps: Positive/success-shell tid space beyond the current `431/453/476/483/506/537` family map
Whether broader reserved-looking extra keys besides the tested set change behavior
Whether the current API1 shell families drift under aged-cookie or login-state replay

otype.observed_role: controls response wrapper; only exact lowercase `json` switches to JSONP

branch_examples.result: falls back to XML rather than JSONP
falls back to XML rather than JSONP

notes: When a single `otype` key is exact lowercase `json`, both success and failure stay inside the JSONP wrapper.
Current same-day evidence says JSONP changes wrapper/content-type, not the tested batch semantics.
Repeated `otype` currently behaves as first-value-wins in the tested canonical anonymous branch.
In the tested JSONP branch, adding `callback=1` replaces the default `QZOutputJson=` assignment wrapper with a raw callback-style `1({...})` wrapper while preserving the JSON payload semantics.
open_questions: No evidence yet for any positive wrapper value besides XML default and lowercase JSONP.

tid.observed_role: request routing / config selector; `535` is the current canonical rich-detail branch, while later anonymous evidence now separates API2 into richer and thinner positive shells plus a near-empty success shell rather than one flat positive class

failing_examples.errormsg: 错误的tid, 如果新申请的接口,请等待10分钟
错误的tid, 如果新申请的接口,请等待10分钟
错误的tid, 如果新申请的接口,请等待10分钟

notes: Repeated `tid` currently behaves as first-value-wins in the tested canonical anonymous branch.

Later caller-facing validation now distinguishes `488` as a thinner alternate positive shell, `502` as a richer alternate positive shell, `506` as a near-empty success shell, `540` as another thinner positive shell, and `541` as an even thinner positive shell relative to canonical `535`.
Under anonymous real-cookie replay, the tested auth-ish extra-key semantics stayed unchanged across the currently confirmed positive API2 tid cluster `535/540/541`.
The later anonymous 641-647 jump-scan did not reveal a new positive API2 cluster; the only new wrinkle was a local `tid=642 -> -111013` error-family branch on the second sample.
open_questions: Whether API2 has another positive or success-shell tid beyond the current `488/502/506/535/540/541` family map is still open.

appid.observed_role: numeric canonical appid enables stricter appkey validation; missing / text / `1` currently bypass that stricter branch
notes: `appid=20001238` is the current canonical numeric appid in repo examples and probes.
Repeated `appid` currently behaves as first-value-wins in the tested bad-appkey collision controls between the canonical numeric branch and the text-bypass branch.
The searched anonymous cutovers are now tightened to `20001000 -> appid no find`, `20001001 -> appkey error`, `20002581 -> appkey error`, and `20002582 -> appid no find`.
open_questions: The tested anonymous numeric slice is now partially mapped, but the full numeric appid space is still not closed outside the searched cutovers and bands.

appkey.observed_role: validated when the canonical numeric appid branch is active
working_examples: paired with `appid=20001238` and the canonical appkey
omitted when appid is missing or text
notes: Repeated `appkey` currently behaves as first-value-wins in the tested canonical-appid branch when good and wrong appkey values collide.
Current anonymous numeric evidence still does not show that the canonical appkey satisfies the searched near-canonical appid family `20001001-20002581`; those values remain on `appkey error`.
open_questions: No evidence yet that appkey unlocks any API2 capability beyond satisfying the exact canonical numeric-appid branch.

union_platform.observed_role: present in canonical examples but not validated by the current tested branch set
notes: Current evidence says the anonymous canonical branch and the later real anonymous visitor-cookie replay both did not show a visible behavioral split on union_platform.
This is still a black-box conclusion limited to same-day anonymous environments plus the real anonymous visitor-cookie replay.
Repeated `union_platform=3&999` versus `999&3` showed no observed change in the tested XML canonical branch, but that does not prove first-value-wins.
open_questions: Whether union_platform matters under aged-cookie or real login-state replay remains open.

failing_examples.errormsg: idlist个数错误, 为0或超过限制

batch_semantics: Maximum tested batch size is 32 non-empty items.
Duplicate items count toward the 32-item limit.
Invalid items count toward the 32-item limit.
Mixed valid + invalid batches keep order and degrade bad items into empty-shell rows instead of top-level failure.
Repeated query-key form such as `idlist=a&idlist=b` currently behaves as first-value-wins in the tested canonical anonymous branch.
open_questions: No evidence yet that API2 merges repeated `idlist` keys across query-string positions.

candidate_hidden_parameters.name: callback
candidate_hidden_parameters.observed_role: replaces the default `QZOutputJson=` assignment wrapper with a callback-style wrapper for a wide tested callback family, while the empty callback value falls back to the default assignment shell and some unmatched-delimiter values produce parse-breaking wrappers

current_non_goals: No strong repository evidence yet for versioned or auth-token query parameters on the two main API2 wrappers; beyond the confirmed API2 JSONP `callback` wrapper surface, the tested reserved-looking keys `format/output/version/v/platform/source` stayed unchanged on the anonymous canonical branches and the later real anonymous visitor-cookie replay, while the tested auth-ish keys `token/sign/sig/appver/access_token/authkey/openid` also stayed unchanged under the later real anonymous visitor-cookie replay.
priority_gaps: Positive/success-shell tid space beyond the current `488/502/506/535/540/541` family map
Login-state or aged-cookie effect on union_platform, now that live anonymous visitor-cookie replay is closed
API2 JSONP callback behavior for parser-breaking punctuation beyond the tested empty/numeric/identifier/dotted/dash/bracket/dollar/space/comma/Unicode/close-paren/comment-like set
Whether auth-leaning or session-coupled extra keys change behavior outside the now-closed anonymous plus real-anonymous replay branches, especially under login-state or aged-cookie replay

notes: Current repo evidence for the two main endpoints only consistently exposes API1: `tid,idlist,appid,appkey` and API2: `otype,tid,appid,appkey,union_platform,idlist`.

Current evidence also directly confirms one auxiliary wrapper surface outside that main key set: API2 JSONP `callback`, which changes the wrapper prefix across a wide tested value family while preserving the payload family, except that the empty callback value falls back to the default shell.

The next highest-value closure work is not broad hidden-parameter hunting but login-state/aged-cookie replay, broader positive tid discovery beyond the now-confirmed API1 `431/453/476/483/506/537` and API2 `488/502/506/535/540/541` family maps, callback punctuation probing, and replaying the tested auth-ish keys outside the now-closed anonymous plus real-anonymous visitor-cookie branches.

components.name: tencent_cookie_env_from_headers.py
components.role: convert raw desktop/mobile Cookie headers into replay env JSON for real / aged / login-state runs

components.name: tencent_environment_matrix_probe.py
components.role: run the canonical contract matrix across replay header environments

components.name: tencent_param_semantics_probe.py
components.role: replay auth-ish and callback/repeated-key semantics under a chosen env; now also supports dynamic callback-tail values via `--extra-callback-value / --extra-callback-file`

components.name: tencent_replay_bundle_runner.py
components.role: one-command orchestration for env-json build + matrix + semantics, now supporting `--semantics-profile full`, callback-tail forwarding via `--probe-extra-callback-value / --probe-extra-callback-file`, plus writable final/staging directories via `--artifact-output-dir / --subprocess-output-dir`

current_proven_scope: anonymous real-cookie replay path is already closed
replay bundle callback-support and artifact-output-dir staging are self-validated
aged-cookie and login-state still need real external Cookie input before the parameter conclusions themselves can be upgraded
